mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive forward returns for the S&P 500 over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in less than 50% of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 65% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which directly boosts corporate revenues and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Interaction term loses statistical significance with p-value greater than 0.10 in multivariate regression or joint positive signals yield negative returns in greater than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing and utility output, which translates to higher near-term corporate cash flows and positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during late-cycle phases, producing negative returns in greater than 60% of triggered instances when annual INDPRO growth exceeds 1.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of federal funds rates below 0.5 percent and stable unemployment creates a favorable macro environment where cheap capital sustains equity valuations while labor markets prevent recession, yielding positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Joint condition fails to predict returns with Rank IC greater than 0.04 or positive returns drop below 50% when unemployment rises concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth falling below the 1.5 percent threshold indicates weakening aggregate demand that compresses profit margins and reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 60% of sub-1.5% growth quarters over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrate expanding labor demand, which supports household income growth and consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 across all rolling 3-year windows or payroll growth shows no significant correlation with 20-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden contraction in industrial production exceeding 0.5 percent month-over-month signals a supply-side shock or demand collapse that forces corporate earnings downgrades, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Negative returns occur in less than 50% of triggered instances or the signal produces false positives in greater than 70% of non-recessionary periods.
